cmp99.title: Propagators for Lattice Gauge Field Theories in a Background Field III
cmp99.author: T. Balaban
cmp99.bibliography_evidence: Dimock I reference [9], arXiv:1108.1335, printed page 50; Dimock II reference [7], printed page 92.
cmp99.license_note: Keep primary PDF in the private source packet; commit only metadata, formulas, locators and short excerpts.

citations.summary: Intermediate large-field C_k^(n) term bound visually checked at Eq. (1.70); still needs the full symbol dictionary before theorem use.

formulas.statement: For each term C_k^(n)(X, U_k^(n)) in the representation (1.63), the analytic extension on the stated U-bar space satisfies the large-field bound.

open_questions: Exact quantifiers and hypotheses of representation (1.63), including the U-bar analyticity space.
Source-to-Lean dictionary for X in D_m, d_m(X), and the component activity to which C_k^(n) contributes.
How this intermediate C_k^(n) bound feeds the final post-R activity rather than a pre-R surrogate.

citations.summary: Exponentiated R' cluster expansion, local bound, and second-group post-R bound visually checked at Eqs. (1.98)--(1.100); still needs the activity dictionary.

formulas.statement: The exponentiated cluster expansion is written as exp R'^(k) = exp of the sum over localized R'^(k)(X) terms.
The localized R'^(k)(X) term has an analytic extension and satisfies the page's pre-split bound with c_1.
For the second group, whose localization domains are disjoint from the indicated large-field region, c_1 is exp(-p_0(g_k)) and the linear size in (1.99) is replaced by d_k(X).

open_questions: Full Theorem 1 hypotheses and parameter restrictions for the exponentiated expansion.
Exact dictionary from first-group R' terms to new boundary terms B'^(k)(X), and from second-group terms to final R terms.
Source-to-Lean dictionary for d_{k, union Y_i}(X), d_k(X), admissible domains, and the repository PhysicalGaugeLocalActivity/LocalActivity.finsetSum conventions.
How Eq. (1.101) and the following induction-hypothesis statement instantiate the repository post-R physical activity.

citations.summary: Dedicated extraction target for uniform Green/operator bounds and localized exponential decay currently recorded only narratively.
open_questions: Exact paper title, proposition numbering and quantifiers.
Exact conversion from G_k bounds to the repository C_k covariance convention.
Uniformity in scale, volume, lattice spacing and boundary conditions.

citations.summary: Locate and extract the one-step covariance law, normalization and scale dictionary.
Extract the page-19/20 formulas (14)-(15) and map every symbol to the repository.
open_questions: Exact formula transcription and surrounding definitions.

citations.summary: Locate the background-field propagator theorem, decay estimates, hypotheses and scale dictionary needed by the physical covariance campaign.
open_questions: Dictionary to the repository physical gauge bonds and Hessian.

citations.summary: Exact Appendix-F cluster expansion with holes: theorem hypotheses, Ω-connectivity convention, first connected activity, metric stitching, ultralocal integration and second Ursell exponentiation.
extracted_claims: Theorem F.1 works on a unit lattice with Ω and Λ unions of M-cubes and Λ contained in Ω.
The measure μ_Λ is ultralocal and makes the integrated field variables independent.
Ω-connectedness is intersection through the active region Ω; Ω-disjoint polymers need not be disjoint as full sets.
H# has local influence: H#(Y) depends only on H(X) with X contained in Y.

formulas.ascii: |H#(Y,Phi_prime)| <= O(1) * H0 * exp(-(kappa - 3*kappa0 - 3) * d_M(Y, mod Omega^c))
formulas.conclusion: Xi = exp(sum_Y H#(Y,Phi_prime)), with Y intersect Lambda nonempty.

dictionary_links.statement: X1 and X2 are Ω-connected iff X1 ∩ X2 ∩ Ω is nonempty.
dictionary_links.blocker: Prove all source-domain and metric identifications for the concrete Yang-Mills activity.

use_for: Canonical source anchor for the complete Appendix-F-with-holes proof architecture.
Exact loss of 3*kappa0+3 in the final H# decay.
Exact Ω-connectedness convention and ultralocal integration step.
do_not_use_for: Do not identify scalar-model activities with Yang-Mills activities without a model-specific dictionary.
Do not replace the missing connected-cover entropy proof merely by citing the final bound.
Do not replace Ω-connectivity by ordinary overlap or touching without a comparison theorem.
open_questions: Exact concrete Yang-Mills activity satisfying Eq. (634).
Repository theorem closing the connected-cover entropy step behind Eq. (642).
Measurable/type-local realization of K, K# and H# for the physical field types.

coverage.next_action: Locate primary PDF and extract (1.89), (1.114), Prop. 1.1/1.2.

coverage.formula_status: Appendix F and major RG interfaces extracted

coverage.formula_status: Theorem 1 local bounds and final stability extracted
coverage.next_action: Use only as scalar-model architecture; identify which local E/R/B bounds have a legitimate Yang-Mills analogue.
